answer: Krasna et al. reported a 73% incidence of metastasis in patients with perineural invasion, as compared with 27% in those without such invasion (P <.01) and a 30% survival rate versus 58%, respectively (P <.003). Several other authors have confirmed that perineural invasion can be considered an independent prognostic factor for survival, whereas Bouvet et al. failed to demonstrate that perineural invasion is a significant prognostic factor for recurrence in treated rectal cancer.
